title: Coupling and decoupling of bilayer graphene monitored by electron energy loss spectroscopy
type1_summary: The Type 1 files are small, structured numeric TXT tables that look replot-ready or nearly replot-ready, despite imperfect headers. They are consistent with cleaned analysis/source-data style files.
type2_summary: The Type 2 files include the large ZIP container, binary NumPy object arrays, and large matrix-like TXT files with run-style names and nonphysical headers. These are more consistent with raw or intermediate measurement/analysis outputs than cleaned figure tables.
classification_notes: The paper itself is about raw STEM/EELS measurements, but the inspected files are mostly analysis-support files rather than direct microscopy image/spectrum exports. The strongest ambiguity is the 570D*.txt group: they are tabular, but their matrix-like structure and nonphysical headers make them safer to treat as Type 2 rather than cleaned figure data.

paper_analysis.summary: This paper studies bilayer graphene (BLG) using aberration-corrected STEM and monochromated low-loss EELS to correlate twist angle, Moiré superlattices, and optical absorption features arising from van Hove singularity (vHS) transitions. It shows that the vHS-related absorption peak shifts systematically with twist angle and disappears when metal chlorides (CuCl2, AlCl3, FeCl3) are intercalated, demonstrating interlayer decoupling of BLG.

figures.description: Schematic of graphene Brillouin zones for two twist angles and the corresponding momentum-space shift of K points.
Schematic band structure of twisted BLG showing Dirac cone overlap, hybridization, flat bands, and optical transitions EA/EB.
Collection of low-loss EELS spectra from BLG at different twist angles (1–30°), showing twist-dependent EA and EB peaks and a nearly constant pi-plasmon peak.
ADF image of AB-stacked bilayer graphene.
ADF images of twisted BLG at multiple twist angles (1.5° to 30°) showing Moiré superlattices.
ADF image of CuCl2-intercalated BLG (CuCl2@BLG) showing intercalant structure in the vdW gap.
Top and side atomic model of CuCl2 intercalation structure.
Top and side atomic model of AlCl3 intercalation structure.
Top and side atomic model of FeCl3 intercalation structure.
Schematic cross-sectional view of BLG with an intercalant in the vdW gap.
ADF image of the edge of half-filled CuCl2@BLG with a filtered inset showing the graphene domain and Moiré pattern.
Low-loss EELS spectra comparing twisted BLG and CuCl2@BLG, plus AlCl3@BLG and FeCl3@BLG, highlighting disappearance of the vHS peak upon intercalation.

paper_analysis.raw_measurement_details: The paper reports raw STEM ADF images and raw low-loss EELS spectra acquired at room temperature using aberration-corrected JEOL JEM-ARM200F-based microscopes. Imaging was done at 60 kV with specified probe currents and detector angles; EELS used a monochromated electron source with 45 meV energy resolution. A line-scan EELS dataset across a BLG/intercalant boundary is also described.
paper_analysis.processed_plot_details: The paper presents processed/curated spectra and images in the main figures: multiple EELS spectra selected from more than 80 measurements across twist angles, color-coded and arranged to show systematic peak shifts; filtered ADF images/insets; and atomic models for intercalation structures. The spectra appear to be displayed without zero-loss subtraction, but are still curated figure presentations rather than raw file dumps.

dataset_characterization.data_availability_statement: The Supporting Information is available free of charge on the ACS Publications website. It includes schematic descriptions of optical transitions EA and EB, discussion of EELS spatial resolution and delocalization, additional experimental proof that the vHS peak vanishes at CuCl2-intercalated BLG, stability comparison under e-beam, and EELS characterization of surface-deposited vs intercalated AlCl3.
dataset_characterization.raw_data_description: Raw data are described as STEM ADF images and low-loss EELS spectra collected with specific microscopes and acquisition settings. The paper does not mention file formats, repository links, or a public raw-data archive.
dataset_characterization.processed_data_description: Processed data include selected spectra plotted as figure panels, filtered images, and atomic models/schematics. The paper also mentions analysis of more than 80 EELS spectra, but does not provide tabulated extracted peak positions or a numerical dataset in the text.
dataset_characterization.figure_data_description: Figure-specific data are clearly present in the manuscript figures, especially the EELS spectra and ADF images. However, the text does not state that separate source-data files are provided for each figure.
dataset_characterization.scripts_description: No analysis scripts, notebooks, or code are mentioned.
dataset_characterization.data_organization: figure-based in the manuscript; supplementary information for additional examples; no explicit raw-data folder structure specified
dataset_characterization.replot_reason: The paper does not provide explicit source-data files, numerical tables, or scripts in the text. While the figures are based on measured spectra/images, the manuscript alone is not sufficient for direct replotting without digitization or access to underlying files.
dataset_characterization.notes: The paper emphasizes qualitative and semi-quantitative spectral trends rather than releasing a structured dataset. It distinguishes coupled BLG, intercalated BLG, and surface-deposited metal chlorides by EELS signatures and ADF contrast, but the underlying data packaging is not described. Supporting Information likely contains additional figure-level evidence, but not an explicit data repository.

classification_prior.raw_data_evidence: The methods explicitly state acquisition of STEM ADF images and low-loss EELS spectra using JEOL microscopes with defined operating conditions.
classification_prior.source_data_evidence: The text mentions figures and Supporting Information, but does not mention source-data files, numerical tables, or a data repository.
classification_prior.data_availability_section_relevant: Supporting Information is available free of charge on the ACS Publications website; includes schematic description of EA and EB, EELS spatial resolution and delocalization, additional experimental proof of vHS peak vanishing at CuCl2-intercalated BLG, stability difference under e-beam, and EELS characterization of surface-deposited and intercalated AlCl3.
classification_prior.review_notes: This is primarily an experimental microscopy/spectroscopy paper. The manuscript clearly contains raw measurements used to generate figures, but there is no explicit statement of downloadable source data or scripts. Treat figure panels as curated outputs from raw STEM/EELS measurements, not as standalone source-data releases.
